2606.19950.title: Confidence Calibration for Multimodal LLMs: An Empirical Study through Medical VQA
2606.19950.paper_summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) show great potential in medical tasks, but their elicited confidence often misaligns with actual accuracy, potentially leading to misdiagnosis or overlooking correct advice. This study presents the first comprehensive analysis of the relationship between accuracy and confidence in medical MLLMs. It proposes a novel method that combines Multi-Strategy Fusion-Based Interrogation (MS-FBI) with auxiliary expert LLM assessment, aiming to improve confidence calibration in Medical Visual Question Answering (VQA). Experiments demonstrate that our method reduces the Expected Calibration Error (ECE) by an average of 40\% across three Medical VQA datasets, significantly enhancing MLLMs' reliability. The findings highlight the importance of domain-specific calibration for MLLMs in healthcare, offering a more trustworthy solution for AI-assisted diagnosis.
2606.19950.paper_summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）在醫療任務中顯示出巨大的潛力，但它們所引發的信心往往與實際準確性不一致，可能導致誤診或忽略正確建議。這項研究呈現了對醫療 MLLMs 中準確性與信心之間關係的首次全面分析。它提出了一種新方法，將多策略融合基礎的詢問（MS-FBI）與輔助專家 LLM 評估相結合，旨在改善醫療視覺問答（VQA）中的信心校準。實驗表明，我們的方法在三個醫療 VQA 數據集上平均降低了 40\% 的預期校準誤差（ECE），顯著提升了 MLLMs 的可靠性。研究結果強調了在醫療保健中對 MLLMs 進行特定領域校準的重要性，為 AI 輔助診斷提供了更值得信賴的解決方案。
2606.19950.author: Yuetian Du et.al.
2606.19950.authors: Yuetian Du, Yucheng Wang, Ming Kong, Tian Liang, Qiang Long, Bingdi Chen, Qiang Zhu
